name: Warrior
text: Shield-line. Stands where you put them, which is the whole art: the flats are won by whoever refuses to move first.

talents.name: Hold-fast
talents.text: A shout with weight in it. Nearby beasts turn on the shield, and the shield is ready for them.

talents.name: Shield-wall
talents.text: One shield is a wall if you stand close enough to it. Adjacent allies take less.

talents.name: Breakwater
talents.text: Once a fight, the wave breaks on THEM. A small, mortal Call-the-Squall, made of shoulders.

name: Archer
text: Overwatch. Kills what the warrior holds — patience with a string on it.

talents.name: Marked prey
talents.text: What the shield holds, the string finishes. Extra damage against taunted foes.

talents.name: Long eye
talents.text: The flats hide nothing from someone who grew up reading them. Longer reach; the lurking things show themselves.

talents.name: Storm of shafts
talents.text: Once a fight, every arrow they have been saving arrives at the same time.

name: Acolyte
$comment: channelStrength magnitudes are the FRACTION of the patron invocation's effect the Acolyte channels. Part II (Godhead, UNIMPLEMENTED) will multiply these live: effective = base x channelStrength x godhead. Until godhead_system exists the multiplier is 1.0.
text: A lens for their patron — village-scale god-magic. They channel, not cast: the god does the work, the Acolyte does the kneeling.

talents.name: Lesser invocation
talents.text: A weak copy of the patron's first invocation, poured through mortal hands.

talents.name: Patron's ward
talents.text: The god's passive blessing, spread thin across the party like a shared cloak.

talents.name: Intercession
talents.text: Once a fight, the patron leans all the way in: their second invocation, at the Acolyte's asking.
